segments.text: If you ever walk into an IKEA, it's
almost impossible to leave, and that is
on purpose. Their whole store is a giant
trap. Normal stores have straight
aisles, but IKEA is built like a massive
one-way maze. So, you literally have to
walk past everything they sell. But you
getting trapped, isn't that annoying?
Actually, no. When you get a little
lost, you lose track of time and you
basically enter this relaxing shopping
trance and you end up spending more
money when you're lost. But how so? It's
actually a sneaky mind trick. When your
brain feels confused, it wants a quick
reward to feel in control again. So, you
start grabbing cheap stuff. You did not
go there for them, but tossing them in
your cart gives you a little hit of
dopamine. Isn't that clever? For more
clever designs, subscribe.
